_comment: §9.2 α=4 variant of run_arch_ssd_band_rrs. D=64 fixed; P bumped from 64 to 256 (α = P/D = 4) to move out of the finite-sample ridge regime where the original α=1 run floored at ~0.28. Same spectral construction, same theorem-C E⋆ values (those are population-level and don't depend on P). Feature-space forward pass is mandatory at P=256 — the refactored ReducedGammaSpectralFilter avoids building the (B, P, P) matrix, keeping per-step cost O(L·D·P) (linear in P) ≈ 2.5 ms/step. The α=1 run at outputs/thesis/architectures/run_arch_ssd_band_rrs/run_arch_ssd_band_rrs-20260414T233703Z-5b368495/ validates block-scalar SYMMETRY; this α=4 run tests whether theorem-C LOSS-MAGNITUDES emerge as the sample operator concentrates.
plan_reference: EXPERIMENT_PLAN_FINAL.MD §9.2 (α=4 follow-up to the α=1 canonical run)
